{"key_event":"1863 Constitution of Rionegro","reasoning":"Radical federalism, broad liberties, armed partisan organization, and effective congressional resistance to Mosquera raised organized society’s ability to constrain the executive, though much of this power belonged to regional elites and militias rather than inclusive civic associations. The national state’s already weak reach declined further after civil war and the Rionegro settlement, despite some secularizing capacity against the Church and the continued authority of state-level governments.","society_change":0.7,"society_power":6.5,"state_change":-0.4,"state_power":2.8}
